subject: Quick question on transition workflows
email_text:
Hi Greg,

I have been following your work at GA Sears Coaching & Leadership Development, and I thought this might be useful for the executives you support.

Starting Monday gives executive coaches a hard-edged execution layer: company targeting, interview narrative discipline, and outreach workflows that hold up under pressure.

For COACH clients at GA Sears Coaching & Leadership Development, the failure mode is looking qualified on paper but losing signal quality in live conversations.

In recent executive coaching engagements, progression improved when first-touch messaging was rewritten for role-specific decision criteria.

The concrete proof point is a role narrative that survives hard follow-up questions without drifting into generic language.

If this is not relevant right now, no worries.

If useful, I can send the executive signal-quality interview map so you can see exactly how it changes first-touch conversation quality.

If useful, If helpful, I can send a one-page example. If not useful right now, no worries.

Rich
